key_insight: The paper introduces a fully variational CutFEM for finite-strain elasticity where the total energy, weak boundary enforcement, and ghost stabilization generate the residual and tangent consistently via automatic differentiation.
engineering_relevance: It enables robust unfitted finite-strain simulations on complex geometries with cut-independent conditioning and reusable hyperelastic model definitions, which is valuable for practical solid mechanics workflows.
method: Augmented energy = bulk hyperelastic energy + Nitsche boundary terms + ghost-penalty stabilization
Residual and symmetrised tangent are obtained by successive variations; AD computes stress and elasticity tensors from the scalar energy density
Cut-independent coercivity/continuity and an O(h^-2) condition bound support quasi-optimal Newton solves
evidence_signal: The abstract reports optimal h-convergence for linear, quadratic, and cubic elements on a smooth test case, and shows that corner singularities cap convergence until local refinement restores the optimal rate.
watch_item: Accuracy can still be limited by mixed Dirichlet–Neumann corner singularities, so mesh refinement near junctions is important in practice.
paper_title: A Unified CutFEM Formulation for Finite-Strain Elasticity: Energy Minimisation and Corner Singularities
